title: Jaguars' Coen: Staff "less frantic" in Year 2
body:
As the NFL draft approaches, the Jacksonville Jaguars are poised to make significant strides in their second year under the leadership of head coach Liam Coen and General Manager James Gladstone. The duo, who took the reins last year, have had a full season to settle into their roles and are now better equipped to tackle the challenges of building a winning team. With a year of experience under their belts, Coen and Gladstone are exuding a sense of calm and confidence, a stark contrast to the frantic pace they faced in their inaugural season. This newfound composure is expected to pay dividends as they navigate the intricacies of the draft, making informed decisions that will shape the future of the franchise.

The first year in any new role is often marked by a steep learning curve, and Coen and Gladstone were no exception. As they navigated the complexities of their positions, they had to adapt quickly to the demands of the NFL. The draft, in particular, is a high-pressure event that requires meticulous planning and execution. Last year, the Jaguars' staff was still finding its footing, and the sense of urgency was palpable. However, with a year of experience and a deeper understanding of the team's needs, Coen and Gladstone are now more focused on the task at hand. They have had the opportunity to assess the team's strengths and weaknesses, identify areas for improvement, and develop a clear strategy for the draft.

One of the key benefits of having a second year under their belts is that Coen and Gladstone have been able to establish a stronger rapport with their staff and players. This increased familiarity has enabled them to build a more cohesive unit, with everyone working towards a common goal. The coach and GM have also had the chance to evaluate the team's performance over the course of a full season, gaining valuable insights into what works and what doesn't. This knowledge will be invaluable as they make key decisions during the draft, allowing them to target players who fit their system and address specific needs.

The Jaguars' front office has been busy in the offseason, making moves to bolster the roster and create a more competitive team. With the draft approaching, Coen and Gladstone will be looking to add talented young players who can contribute to the team's success. The team's needs are well-documented, and the coach and GM have a clear idea of what they are looking for in terms of skillset and fit. As they prepare to make their selections, they will be drawing on the knowledge and experience gained over the past year, using it to inform their decisions and build a stronger, more balanced roster.

As the draft gets underway, all eyes will be on the Jaguars, eager to see how Coen and Gladstone will use their newfound experience and confidence to shape the team's future. The NFL is a league of constant change, and the ability to adapt and evolve is crucial for success. The Jaguars' leadership duo has demonstrated a willingness to learn and grow, and their increased sense of calm and composure is a positive sign for the team's prospects. With a clear plan in place and a deeper understanding of the team's needs, Coen and Gladstone are well-positioned to make a significant impact in the draft and take the Jaguars to the next level.

As the NFL draft comes to a close, the real work will begin for Coen, Gladstone, and the rest of the Jaguars' staff. The team will be looking to integrate its new players into the roster, and the coach and GM will be working tirelessly to ensure a smooth transition. With their second year underway, the Jaguars are poised to take a significant step forward, and the future looks bright for this emerging franchise. As the team embarks on a new season, one thing is clear: Coen and Gladstone are ready to put their experience to the test, and the results will be worth watching.
